english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Katie is a human being. Therefore, Katie is smarter than a chimpanzee.
ugf_translation: Find if each reason is good or not good. If not good, show a case that makes it wrong. The person we talk about is a human being. So the person we talk about is more thinking than the animal that lives in trees and looks like a small human.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Bob is a fireman. Therefore, Bob has put out fires.
ugf_translation: Find out if each reason is good or not good. If not good, show a case that makes it wrong. The person is a person who works with water to stop fire. So, the person has stopped fire.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Gerald is a mathematics professor. Therefore, Gerald knows how to teach mathematics.
ugf_translation:
Check if each reason is right or wrong. If wrong, give a thing that shows it can be not right.

The person who works as a teacher of the study of numbers at a school for higher learning is a teacher of the study of numbers. So the person knows how to teach the study of numbers.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Monica is a French teacher. Therefore, Monica knows how to teach French.
ugf_translation:
Find out if each group of words that try to show something is good or not good. If not good, give a small case that shows it does not work.

The person who works as a teacher of the language from the country with the tall metal shape. So the person who works as a teacher of the language from the country with the tall metal shape knows how to show that language to other people.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Bob is taller than Susan. Susan is taller than Frankie. Therefore, Bob is taller than Frankie.
ugf_translation:
Find out if each reason is good or not good. If not good, give a case that shows it wrong.
The first person is more tall than the second person. The second person is more tall than the third person. So the first person is more tall than the third person.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Craig loves Linda. Linda loves Monique. Therefore, Craig loves Monique.
ugf_translation:
Find out if each reason is good or bad. If it is bad, show a case that makes it not good.

the first person loves the second person. the second person loves the third person. So the first person loves the third person.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Orel Hershizer is a Christian. Therefore, Orel Hershizer communicates with God.
ugf_translation:
Find out if each reason is good or not good. If not good, show a case that makes it not work.
The person is the person who follows the group that believes in the big maker. So the person talks with the big maker.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

All Muslims pray to Allah. Muhammad is a Muslim. Therefore, Muhammad prays to Allah.
ugf_translation:
find out whether each reasoning is true or not true. if not true, give a different situation that shows it is not true.

all people who believe in the god that they call the one who made everything talk to the god. the man who believes in the god is a person who believes in the god. so, the man who believes in the god talks to the god.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Some protozoa are predators. No protozoa are animals. Therefore, some predators are not animals.
ugf_translation: Find out if each set of words is true or not true. If not true, show a small case that makes it not true. Some tiny water life are the kind of thing that eats other things. No tiny water life are animals. So some the kind of thing that eats other things are not animals.

english_source:
Determine whether each argument is valid or invalid. If invalid, give a counterexample.

Charlie only barks when he hears a burglar outside. Charlie is barking. Therefore, there must be a burglar outside.
ugf_translation: Find out if each reason is good or not good. If not good, give a case that makes it wrong. The man only makes a dog sound when he hears a person who takes things outside. The man is making a dog sound. So there is a person who takes things outside.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Ed rides horses. Therefore, Ed is a cowboy.
ugf_translation: Give the missing idea that makes the last idea work. The man rides horses. So, the man is the man who works with horses on a big land.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Tom was driving over the speed limit. Therefore, Tom was doing something wrong.
ugf_translation: Driving over the most how fast allowed is wrong.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

If it is raining then the ground is wet. Therefore, the ground must be wet.
ugf_translation: It is raining. If it is raining then the ground is wet. So the ground is wet.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

All elves drink Guinness, which is why Olaf drinks Guinness.
ugf_translation: All small story people with long ears drink the dark drink, so the small story person with long ears drink the dark drink.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Mark didn’t invite me to homecoming. Instead, he invited his friend Alexia. So he must like Alexia more than me. 31
ugf_translation:
Give the missing thought that helps the ending work.

Mark did not ask me to go to the school dance. He asked his friend the girl he knows better than me to go instead. So he must like the girl he knows better than he likes me.

Missing thought: If a person likes one person more than another, they will ask the more liked person to go to the dance.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

The watch must be broken because every time I have looked at it, the hands have been in the same place.
ugf_translation: If the thing that shows time is not broken, its hand parts would move. The thing that shows time must be broken because every time I have looked at it, its hand parts have been in the same place.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Olaf drank too much Guinness and fell out of his second story apartment window. Therefore, drinking too much Guinness caused Olaf to injure himself.
ugf_translation: the thought: if a person falls out of a high window they get hurt. the man drank too much the dark brown drink made from the plant that grows in the ground and fell out of the window on the second floor of his home. so drinking too much the dark brown drink made from the plant that grows in the ground made the man get hurt.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Mark jumped into the air. Therefore, Mark landed back on the ground.
ugf_translation: All things that go up must go down.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

In 2009 in the United States, the net worth of the median white household was $113,149 a year, whereas the net worth of the median black household was $5,677. Therefore, as of 2009, the United States was still a racist nation.
ugf_translation: If a group of people has much less money than another group of people because of the color of their skin, then the place where they live is still a place that acts on people not the same because of skin color. In 2009 in the big country of many people, the middle white family had about 113,149, while the middle black family had about 5,677. So, the missing idea is that the big space between the amounts of money shows that the country still acts on people not the same because of skin color.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

The temperature of the water is 212 degrees Fahrenheit. Therefore, the water is boiling.
ugf_translation: The water is at the number two one two on the hot way people count heat. So the water is the hot water that makes the little round pieces of air and turns to the hot water gas.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Capital punishment sometimes takes innocent lives, such as the lives of individuals who were later found to be not guilty. Therefore, we should not allow capital punishment.
ugf_translation: the idea that if a thing sometimes takes the lives of people who did not do a bad thing, then we should not let that thing happen. the big final kill by the state sometimes takes the lives of people who did not do a bad thing, such as the lives of people who later were found to have not done a bad thing. so we should not let the big final kill by the state.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Allowing immigrants to migrate to the U.S. will take working class jobs away from working class folks. Therefore, we should not allow immigrants to migrate to the U.S.
ugf_translation: If we let people who move from one country to another go to the big country with many states, then the jobs that people who work for pay have will be taken away from those people. So we should not let those people go to the big country with many states.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Prostitution is a fair economic exchange between two consenting adults. Therefore, prostitution should be allowed.
ugf_translation: The work where an adult person gives body for money is a good give and take between two adult people who both agree. So the work where an adult person gives body for money should be allowed.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Colleges are more interested in making money off of their football athletes than in educating them. Therefore, college football ought to be banned.
ugf_translation: big schools that teach people are more about getting money from the people who play the game with a round ball that is kicked with feet than about teaching them. So, the game with a round ball that is kicked with feet at big schools should be stopped.

english_source:
Supply the missing premise needed for the conclusion to follow.

Edward received an F in college Algebra. Therefore, Edward should have studied more.
ugf_translation: Give the missing reason that makes the ending work. The student got a bad mark in the big school the kind of numbers study with letters. So the student should have done more study.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Although drilling for oil in Alaska will disrupt some wildlife, it is better than having to depend on foreign oil, which has the tendency to draw us into foreign conflicts that we would otherwise not be involved in.
ugf_translation: Find which talking way (making sure, guarding, making less important, or saying if good) is used. Even though making holes in the ground for black water in the cold north will hurt some animals, it is more good than having to need black water from other lands, which can pull us into fights far away that we would not join in a different way.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Let there be no doubt: the entity that carried out this attack is a known terrorist organization, whose attacks have a characteristic style—a style that is seen in this attack today.
ugf_translation: Find which kind of word use (say for sure, keep safe, make seem less, or saying good or bad words) is being used. Let there be no not sure: the thing that did this attack is a known fear group, whose attacks have a different way—a way that is seen in this attack now.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Privatizing the water utilities in Detroit was an unprecedented move that has garnered a lot of criticism. Nonetheless, it is helping Detroit to recover from bankruptcy.
ugf_translation: Find which kind of talk (promise, guard, down play, or value words) is used. Selling the water service to the group of many people in the big city was a never before thing that got many bad talk. Still, it is helping the big city get out of the money trouble.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Most pediatricians agree that the single most important factor in childhood obesity is eating sugary, processed foods, which have become all too common in our day and age.
ugf_translation: Find which kind of word thing (making sure, keeping safe, saying not as good, or words that say good or bad) is used. Most doctors who work with kids say that the single most important thing in kids being too heavy is eating sweet, made‑in‑a‑place where things are made foods, which have become many now.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Although not every case of AIDS is caused by HIV, it is arguable that most are.
ugf_translation: Find which word thing (making sure, keeping safe, putting down, or saying good or bad words) is being used. Even if not all cases of the bad feeling that hurts you are made by the tiny living thing that can make you sick, many people can say that most are.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Abraham Lincoln was probably our greatest president since he helped keep together a nation on the brink of splintering into two.
ugf_translation:
Find which way of using words (making sure, the way of keeping safe, putting down, or the way of saying good or bad) is being used.
The well known leader of long ago was maybe our best leader because he helped hold a country that was almost break into two parts together.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

No one with any sense would support Obamacare.
ugf_translation: Find which kind of word way (the way that tries to make you feel safe, the way that tries to keep you safe, the way that tries to make something seem less, or the way that says something is good or bad) is used. No person with any sense would like the big plan about paying for doctor visit for sick people.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Even if universal healthcare is expensive, it is still the just thing to do. 36
ugf_translation:
Say which kind of talk (sure, keep, lower, or the way of saying good or bad) is used.
Even if body help for all the thing of giving money a lot, it is still the good thing to do. 36

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

While our country has made significant strides in overcoming explicit racist policies, the wide disparity of wealth, prestige and influence that characterize white and black Americans shows that we are still implicitly a racist country.
ugf_translation: Find which kind of word game (saying it will be good, keeping safe, making seem less, or telling if good or bad) is used. Even though our land has made big steps in fixing clear the set of things that tell what to do about the kind of group of people with light skin and the kind of group of people with dark skin, the big space between money, respect and power that the people with light skin and the people with dark skin have shows that we are still a land that is not good about the kind of group of people with skin color in a hidden way.

english_source:
Identify which rhetorical technique (assuring, guarding, discounting, or evaluative language) is being used.

Recent studies have show that there is no direct link between vaccines and autism.
ugf_translation: Find which kind of talking way (saying yes, keeping, lowering, or words that say how good or bad) is used. New study has shown that there is no straight join between shots and the problem where a child has trouble with talking and playing with other people.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Bob, from Silverton, CO drives a 4x4 pickup truck, so most people from Silverton, CO drive 4x4 pickup trucks.
ugf_translation: Show what kind of problem, if any, the number made from a group guess has. The man with the short name from the town with the name that has the words bright metal in it in a state drives a four by four big car, so most people from the town with the name that has the words bright metal in it in a state drive four by four big cars.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every morning from 5:00-5:20 am. He counts mostly Mourning Doves and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree in the morning are Mourning Doves.”
ugf_translation: the kind of problem is a quick guess from a small group of things. the person watches and puts birds into groups that land on the tree in the yard each morning from five time to five twenty. he sees mostly the sad white bird that makes a soft sound and says, “most birds that land on my tree in the morning are the sad white bird that makes a soft sound.”

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every morning from 5:00-6:00 am. He counts mostly Mourning Doves and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree during the 24-hour day are Mourning Doves.” 146
ugf_translation:
find out what kind of trouble, if any, the number big idea has.

the man counts and puts into groups birds that land in the tree in his yard each morning from 5 to 6 am. he counts mostly the small gray bird that makes a soft sound and says, “most birds that land in my tree during the whole day are the small gray bird that makes a soft sound.”

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every day from 5:00-6:00 am, from 11:00-12:00 pm, and from 5:00-6:00 pm. He counts mostly Mourning Doves and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree during the 24-hour day are Mourning Doves.”
ugf_translation: The kind of problem is a guess that is too big for the small look. He only looks at birds that come to his tree at three short times, and most of those are the soft gray birds that say sad sounds. He then says that most birds that come to his tree all day are those sad birds, which is a guess that goes beyond the small look.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every evening from 10:00-11:00 pm. He counts mostly owls and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree throughout the 24-hour day are owls.”
ugf_translation: Find out what sort of problem, if any, the number big idea has. The person who lives in the house counts and puts into groups the birds that sit on the big tree in the place behind the house each time after the sun goes down for one hour. He sees mostly the night bird with a round head and big eyes that can turn its head all the way around and makes a big idea, most birds that sit on my big tree all day are the night bird with a round head and big eyes.
